tweets.text:
DON'T EVEN THINK ABOUT IT! 

Pointing a laser at an aircraft is a serious federal offense and poses a significant threat to aviation safety. This reckless act endangers the lives of pilots, passengers, and individuals on the ground by potentially impairing a pilot’s vision during critical phases of flight. 
 
Under federal law, individuals who aim a laser at an aircraft can face: 
🟤 Fines of up to $250,000 
🟤 Imprisonment for up to 5 years 
🟤Additional FAA civil penalties 
🔴Up to $11,000 per violation: The FAA can impose significant fines for each instance of  aiming  a laser at an aircraft. 
🔴Up to $30,800 for multiple incidents: For individuals who engage in multiple laser incidents, the FAA's total civil penalty can be higher. 
 
Law enforcement agencies, including DHS, take these incidents very seriously and will pursue offenders to the fullest extent of the law!

author.description: Safeguarding the Nation from the air and sea.

tweets.text:
🚨 The Border is Closed 🚨

Last week, a San Diego Air and Marine Air Branch aircrew detected a 30’ panga vessel south of the Maritime Boundary Line (MBL). Through swift coordination with the Joint Harbor Operations Center and Regional Coordinating Mechanism (ReCoM) partners, a UH-60 from the USS Sampson was deployed and reacquired the vessel north of the MBL. 

The UH-60 aircrew vectored in a Long Beach Marine Unit crew, which successfully interdicted the vessel 4 nautical miles from Santa Barbara Island—stopping it without pursuit. Onboard were 15 illegal aliens. All were arrested and transferred to the OFO STRIKE team for processing. 

Let this serve as a strong warning: Our borders are CLOSED. Any attempt to enter the United States illegally will be met with immediate action. Violators will be apprehended and prosecuted. DHS remains steadfast in enforcing the law and safeguarding our nation.
